     The Numbers Business Report

Batman Begins (2005)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $205,343,774Details
International Box Office $151,422,858
Worldwide Box Office $356,766,632
Further financial details...

Synopsis

A young Bruce Wayne travels to the Far East, where he’s trained in the martial arts by Henri Ducard, a member of the mysterious League of Shadows. When Ducard reveals the League’s true purpose — the complete destruction of Gotham City — Wayne returns to Gotham intent on cleaning up the city without resorting to murder. With the help of Alfred, his loyal butler, and Lucius Fox, a tech expert at Wayne Enterprises, Batman is born.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$48,745,440 (23.7% of total gross)
Legs:4.21 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:57.6% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$150,000,000 (worldwide box office is 2.4 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,858 opening theaters/3,858 max. theaters, 6.8 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $383,137,524

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: June 15th, 2005 (Wide) by Warner Bros.
International Releases: June 16th, 2005 (Wide) (New Zealand)
June 17th, 2005 (Wide) (Netherlands)
June 17th, 2005 (Wide) (Australia)
June 17th, 2005 (Wide) (Italy)
June 17th, 2005 (Wide), released as Batman Inicia (Mexico)
Video Release: October 18th, 2005 by Warner Home Video
MPA Rating: PG-13 for intense action violence, disturbing images and some thematic elements.
Running Time: 140 minutes
Franchise: Batman, Dark Knight Trilogy
Cast, crew, or production detail: Visual Effects
Character Appearance: Batman
Film format: IMAX: DMR
Plot point: Corrupt Cops, Hallucinations, Origin Story
Social setting: Film Noir
Source material: D.C. Comics
Subgenre: Action Thriller
Source:Based on Comic/Graphic Novel
Genre:Action
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Super Hero
Production/Financing Companies: Legendary Pictures
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

A young Bruce Wayne travels to the Far East, where he’s trained in the martial arts by Henri Ducard, a member of the mysterious League of Shadows. When Ducard reveals the League’s true purpose — the complete destruction of Gotham City — Wayne returns to Gotham intent on cleaning up the city without resorting to murder. With the help of Alfred, his loyal butler, and Lucius Fox, a tech expert at Wayne Enterprises, Batman is born.
